qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Generated Zod client code executes attacker-controlled OpenAPI defaults on import
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory targets unsanitized `x-enumDescriptions` handling in `@orval/core` enum generation via `getEnumImplementation()` in `packages/core/src/getters/enum.ts`, while this finding exploits `schema.default` emission in `packages/zod/src/index.ts` that becomes a top-level runtime export and executes on generated-module import.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause. The only materialized final finding is a Zod `schema.default` code-generation bug, whereas GHSA-h526-wf6g-67jv is the separate `x-enumDescriptions` enum-generation bug in `@orval/core` fixed by JS-escaping values in `packages/core/src/getters/enum.ts`.
evidence: The session's authoritative final-finding manifest `.judge/input_manifest.json` lists only `/Users/lumi/ziyue/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_4bf2331f/generated-code-injection` as a `qualifying_vuln` final finding.
The advisory JSON says untrusted OpenAPI specs inject code through `x-enumDescriptions`, embedded without proper escaping in `getEnumImplementation()` during const enum generation in `@orval/core`.
The upstream fix commit `9e5d93533904936678ba93b5d20f6bca176a4e1e` modifies only `packages/core/src/getters/enum.ts` to JS-escape `x-enumNames` and `x-enumDescriptions` before enum emission.

The qualifying finding's PoC uses `generated-code-injection/reproduction/malicious.yaml` with a malicious `schema.default`, `generated-code-injection/reproduction/orval.config.ts` with `client: 'zod'`, and import-time execution through `generated-code-injection/reproduction/import-generated.mjs`.

The finding report and PoC report both describe the sink as `packages/zod/src/index.ts` generating a top-level `export const ...Default = ${attacker-controlled expression};`, which is a different source field, package path, and code-emission primitive from the advisory's enum-description root cause.
